initiation.description: Grappling attacks are resolved using an opposed roll of the attacker's Unarmed skill versus the combat skill used by the defender.
initiation.special_effects: If one combatant achieves one or more levels of success over the opponent, they may select an equal number of Special Effects (as with Evade).
initiation.requirements: The grappling limb or appendage must be empty and capable of hooking, sticking, or gripping.

limb.effect: Grappled limb is rendered inoperable while grappled.
limb.examples: Arm: cannot use whatever it's holding. Leg: cannot walk/run.

head_or_torso.effect: Imposes a difficulty penalty of Hard against any skill the opponent attempts.
head_or_torso.note: This applies to ALL skills, not just combat.

sustaining_damage.description: On following turns, the grappler may make unarmed attacks to inflict damage on the grappled body part.
sustaining_damage.damage: Equal to the grappler's Damage Modifier only (crushing, twisting, strangulating). No weapon damage die.
sustaining_damage.defense: The defender may oppose with their own Unarmed or Brawn skill to reduce or block the damage.
sustaining_damage.cost: Costs an Action Point per attack.

breaking_free.roll: Opposed roll: victim's Brawn OR Unarmed vs grappler's choice of Brawn OR Unarmed.
breaking_free.note: Some attackers using Brawn may be so strong that no amount of brute force or cunning technique can overcome their grip.

key_points: Unarmed combat can be used interchangeably with any other weapon style if target is within reach
Must declare intent (striking or grappling) in advance — resolution differs
Unarmed combat can be used defensively to parry weapon attacks — damage reduced by comparative weapon Sizes
Stepping within reach of armed opponent reduces effective Size of opponent's weapon
Human unarmed attacks: Size Small, Reach Touch, Damage 1d3

striking.description: Resolved same as normal weapon attacks.

parrying_weapons_unarmed.description: Not simply blocking — uses subtle techniques: pressing against hand/hilt, stepping close to reduce angular momentum, deflecting thrusts, grabbing weapon on haft or near grip.
requirements: Within unarmed range (crucial — longer reach is dangerous)
